title: eragrostis cernua
intro: Eragrostis cernua, also known as fescue grass, is a type of grass native to the southeastern United States and is characterized by its drooping or nodding seed heads. The medicinal properties of Eragrostis cernua include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and respiratory issues. Eragrostis cernua can be prepared as an herbal infusion or decoction to utilize its medicinal properties, often used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
